info.title: ScrapeBadger Google Scraper
info.description: Dedicated API for scraping Google products (SERP, Maps, News, Hotels, Trends, Jobs, Shopping, Patents, Scholar, Autocomplete, Images, Videos, Finance, AI Mode, Lens, Products).

get.description:
Search Google and get structured results.

Two response modes:

- **full** (default, 2 credits): complete SERP with organic, ads,
  knowledge graph, People Also Ask, AI Overview, local pack, news,
  related searches, inline videos, etc. ~1.5-2s cold, ~1.5ms warm.
- **fast** (1 credit, ~40% faster): lite endpoint (`gbv=1`) returning
  only organic results + related searches. Rich blocks (KG, local,
  AI Overview, news) are **not** returned — request ``mode=full`` for
  those. Auto-upgrades to full if ``ai_overview=true`` is set.

First request in either mode triggers a browser warmup (~15s) to
solve SearchGuard. Subsequent requests reuse the sticky session.

parameters.name: q

schema.description: Search query (supports Google operators like site:, inurl:, intitle:)
schema.title: Q

parameters.description: Search query (supports Google operators like site:, inurl:, intitle:)

parameters.name: gl

schema.description: Country code (e.g. us, gb, de, fr)
schema.title: Gl

parameters.description: Country code (e.g. us, gb, de, fr)

parameters.name: tbs

schema.description: Time-based search filter (e.g. qdr:d for past 24h)
schema.title: Tbs

parameters.description: Time-based search filter (e.g. qdr:d for past 24h)

parameters.name: ai_overview

schema.description: When true, chase Google's deferred AI Overview page_token with a follow-up fetch and merge the result into ai_overview. Adds ~1s and 1 credit when the SERP actually defers the overview.
schema.title: Ai Overview

parameters.description: When true, chase Google's deferred AI Overview page_token with a follow-up fetch and merge the result into ai_overview. Adds ~1s and 1 credit when the SERP actually defers the overview.

parameters.name: mode

schema.description: Response mode. **full** (default): complete SERP with all blocks (organic, ads, knowledge graph, local pack, AI overview, news, related questions, etc). **fast**: lite endpoint via `gbv=1` that returns ONLY organic results + related searches in ~0.6-1s cold (vs 1.5-2s full). Use when you only need organic results and can skip rich features.
schema.title: Mode

parameters.description: Response mode. **full** (default): complete SERP with all blocks (organic, ads, knowledge graph, local pack, AI overview, news, related questions, etc). **fast**: lite endpoint via `gbv=1` that returns ONLY organic results + related searches in ~0.6-1s cold (vs 1.5-2s full). Use when you only need organic results and can skip rich features.

get.description:
Search Google Maps for places.

Returns place names, ratings, addresses, phone numbers, coordinates,
hours, and service options.

Get detailed place information from Google Maps via the protobuf API.

Requires either ``place_id`` (e.g. ``ChIJN1t_tDeuEmsRUsoyG83frY4``) or
``data_id`` (e.g. ``0x89c259617ae5b78b:0xe919ce17bb09920e``). The
data_id is available in the ``data_id`` field of ``/maps/search``
results. Returns name, address, rating, review count, phone, website,
GPS coordinates, categories, and up to 20 photo URLs.

Get reviews for a Google Maps place via the protobuf API.

The ``data_id`` is the hex pair from ``/maps/search`` results
(e.g. ``0x89c259617ae5b78b:0xe919ce17bb09920e``). Supports both
offset-based and token-based pagination.

Get photos for a Google Maps place.

Photos are extracted from the ``/maps/preview/place`` response
(field ``data[6][37]``). Returns up to 20 photo URLs with
configurable width/height via the Google CDN pattern
``lh3.googleusercontent.com/gps-cs-s/{id}=w{w}-h{h}-k-no``.

Get Google Business posts / updates for a Maps place.

**Approach**: searches the Google SERP for the business by ``ludocid``
(derived from the ``data_id``'s low hex part) and parses the knowledge
panel's ``data-attrid="kc:/local:any posts"`` and
``kc:/local:merchant_description`` sections. This is the same data
surface and other SERP-based scrapers use.

The Maps protobuf endpoints (``localposts``, ``batchexecute Qt4aBe``)
return empty for virtually all businesses even when they have posts
in the knowledge panel — Google deprecated those endpoints in favor
of the SERP knowledge panel.

Requires ``data_id`` (``0x...:0x...``), available from ``/maps/search``
results.

parameters.name: data_id

schema.description: Google Maps data ID (0x...:0x...) — from /maps/search results
schema.title: Data Id

parameters.description: Google Maps data ID (0x...:0x...) — from /maps/search results

Get Google News articles by topic category via RSS.
Get trending Google News stories via RSS.

Search Google Hotels with dates and pricing.

Uses browser rendering to access Google Travel Hotels.
Get detailed hotel information and pricing.
Get interest over time for search terms via Google Trends internal API.

parameters.name: gprop

schema.description: Property: web, images, news, froogle, youtube
schema.title: Gprop

parameters.description: Property: web, images, news, froogle, youtube

get.description:
Get interest by region for a search term.

Google's widget has a resolution hierarchy — you can't ask for
country-level data inside a specific country. Passing
``resolution=auto`` (the default) uses whatever resolution the
widget itself returned, which is always valid.

parameters.name: resolution

schema.description: Region granularity. `auto` (default) uses Google's widget default, which is `COUNTRY` for worldwide queries and `REGION` (state/province) for single-country queries. Overriding to `COUNTRY` when `geo` is set returns HTTP 400 from Google — use `REGION`, `DMA`, or `CITY` in that case.
schema.title: Resolution

parameters.description: Region granularity. `auto` (default) uses Google's widget default, which is `COUNTRY` for worldwide queries and `REGION` (state/province) for single-country queries. Overriding to `COUNTRY` when `geo` is set returns HTTP 400 from Google — use `REGION`, `DMA`, or `CITY` in that case.

get.description: Get related topics and queries for a search term.

Get trending searches with related queries via Google's TrendsUi.

Primary path: ``POST /_/TrendsUi/data/batchexecute?rpcids=i0OFE``,
Google's internal RPC backend for the ``/trending`` page. This
endpoint is **not** protected by SearchGuard / BotGuard — no
cookies or anti-abuse token required — and returns the trending
list **with related queries embedded per trend** (10-25 queries
each), the same data that powers the trending UI's expanded view.

Fallback: ``GET /trending/rss?geo=US`` — also cookieless but
returns only title + approximate traffic, no related queries.

Verified working 2026-05-08 (research from a parallel investigation
confirmed 30 rapid bursts from one IP all returned 200, payloads
145 KB - 1 MB).

parameters.name: hours

schema.description: Hours-back window for the trending list. Google supports 24, 48, 168 (= 1 week).
schema.title: Hours

parameters.description: Hours-back window for the trending list. Google supports 24, 48, 168 (= 1 week).

get.description:
Return categorized topic entities from the Google Trends autocomplete API.

Unlike Google Search autocomplete (which returns flat keyword suggestions),
this endpoint returns Knowledge Graph entities each tagged with a `type`
and a machine identifier (`mid`) — the same data that powers the Trends
UI's "Search topic" picker. Each entry includes a direct link into the
Trends explore view for that topic.

parameters.name: q

schema.description: Query prefix to resolve into Trends topics
schema.title: Q

parameters.description: Query prefix to resolve into Trends topics

get.description: Unified Google Trends dispatcher. Pick the shape via `data_type`.

parameters.name: q

schema.description: Search term(s). Up to 5 comma-separated terms for TIMESERIES / GEO_MAP; single term for GEO_MAP_0 / RELATED_TOPICS / RELATED_QUERIES. Also accepts a topic MID.
schema.title: Q

parameters.description: Search term(s). Up to 5 comma-separated terms for TIMESERIES / GEO_MAP; single term for GEO_MAP_0 / RELATED_TOPICS / RELATED_QUERIES. Also accepts a topic MID.

parameters.name: data_type

schema.description: Dispatch on: TIMESERIES (interest over time), GEO_MAP (compared breakdown, multi-query), GEO_MAP_0 (interest by region, single query), RELATED_TOPICS (top + rising topics), RELATED_QUERIES (top + rising queries).
schema.title: Data Type

parameters.description: Dispatch on: TIMESERIES (interest over time), GEO_MAP (compared breakdown, multi-query), GEO_MAP_0 (interest by region, single query), RELATED_TOPICS (top + rising topics), RELATED_QUERIES (top + rising queries).

parameters.name: geo

schema.description: Country / region code (e.g. US, GB, US-CA).
schema.title: Geo

parameters.description: Country / region code (e.g. US, GB, US-CA).

parameters.name: region

schema.description: Geo resolution for GEO_MAP / GEO_MAP_0: COUNTRY / REGION / DMA / CITY. Omit to use the widget's own default.
schema.title: Region

parameters.description: Geo resolution for GEO_MAP / GEO_MAP_0: COUNTRY / REGION / DMA / CITY. Omit to use the widget's own default.

get.description:
Current trending searches with the full Google Trends UI filters.

``geo`` honored by the upstream feed. ``category`` is passed through
as ``cat=<letter>``; when the feed ignores it we still return the
base result set so callers get *something* useful. ``status`` and
``sort`` are applied client-side against the parsed result list.

parameters.name: hours

schema.description: Look-back window in hours. Google's UI supports 4, 24, 48, 168. Passed through to the upstream feed; the public RSS currently ignores this filter.
schema.title: Hours

parameters.description: Look-back window in hours. Google's UI supports 4, 24, 48, 168. Passed through to the upstream feed; the public RSS currently ignores this filter.

parameters.name: category

schema.description: Category filter. Accepts Google's letter codes (``b`` business, ``e`` entertainment, ``m`` health, ``t`` sci/tech, ``s`` sports, ``h`` top stories) or the friendly aliases (``business``, ``entertainment``, ``health``, ``sci_tech``/``technology``, ``sports``, ``top_stories``). ``all`` (default) returns every category.
schema.title: Category

parameters.description: Category filter. Accepts Google's letter codes (``b`` business, ``e`` entertainment, ``m`` health, ``t`` sci/tech, ``s`` sports, ``h`` top stories) or the friendly aliases (``business``, ``entertainment``, ``health``, ``sci_tech``/``technology``, ``sports``, ``top_stories``). ``all`` (default) returns every category.

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Trend state. ``active`` keeps only entries with a non-zero search volume (still surging); ``all`` (default) returns every entry including ended ones.
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Trend state. ``active`` keeps only entries with a non-zero search volume (still surging); ``all`` (default) returns every entry including ended ones.

parameters.name: sort

schema.description: Sort order. ``relevance`` (default) keeps Google's ordering; ``search_volume`` orders by parsed traffic descending; ``title`` sorts alphabetically; ``recency`` falls back to relevance when the feed omits publication timestamps.
schema.title: Sort

parameters.description: Sort order. ``relevance`` (default) keeps Google's ordering; ``search_volume`` orders by parsed traffic descending; ``title`` sorts alphabetically; ``recency`` falls back to relevance when the feed omits publication timestamps.

get.description:
Search Google Jobs.

Two data sources are available via ``mode``:

- ``mode=rpc`` (default) — Google's own Careers SPA RPC. Very fast
  (~300 ms), richly structured (title, apply URL, company,
  locations, responsibilities, qualifications, posted-at,
  experience levels). Scope limited to Google's openings.
- ``mode=serp`` — public Google Jobs SERP (aggregator).

parameters.name: location

schema.description: City / state / region — concatenated with `q` before sending to Google.
schema.title: Location

parameters.description: City / state / region — concatenated with `q` before sending to Google.

parameters.name: country

schema.description: Alias for `gl`; when present overrides it.
schema.title: Country

parameters.description: Alias for `gl`; when present overrides it.

parameters.name: language

schema.description: Alias for `hl`; when present overrides it.
schema.title: Language

parameters.description: Alias for `hl`; when present overrides it.

parameters.name: job_type

schema.description: Employment type — translated into a `chips` filter.
schema.title: Job Type

parameters.description: Employment type — translated into a `chips` filter.

parameters.name: date_posted

schema.description: Posted-date window — translated into a `chips` filter.
schema.title: Date Posted

parameters.description: Posted-date window — translated into a `chips` filter.

parameters.name: ltype

schema.description: Work arrangement — maps onto Google's remote/hybrid/onsite chips.
schema.title: Ltype

parameters.description: Work arrangement — maps onto Google's remote/hybrid/onsite chips.

parameters.name: chips

schema.description: Raw Google chip-filter string (comma-separated). Merged with any structured filters (`job_type`, `date_posted`, `ltype`).
schema.title: Chips

parameters.description: Raw Google chip-filter string (comma-separated). Merged with any structured filters (`job_type`, `date_posted`, `ltype`).

parameters.name: uds

schema.description: Opaque Google filter token harvested from a prior Jobs search URL.
schema.title: Uds

parameters.description: Opaque Google filter token harvested from a prior Jobs search URL.

parameters.name: lrad

schema.description: Search radius around the location (Google accepts a distance in miles).
schema.title: Lrad

parameters.description: Search radius around the location (Google accepts a distance in miles).

parameters.name: next_page_token

schema.description: Opaque token from the previous response's `pagination.next`.
schema.title: Next Page Token

parameters.description: Opaque token from the previous response's `pagination.next`.

parameters.name: mode

schema.description: Data source. ``rpc`` (default, ~300 ms) replays Google's own ``r06xKb`` batchexecute RPC on the Google Careers portal — clean JSON, 20 roles per page, scope = Google's internal openings. ``serp`` uses the public Jobs search vertical (``udm=8``, SERP-embedded, 3rd-party aggregator) and costs more latency because Google gates it behind JS.
schema.title: Mode

parameters.description: Data source. ``rpc`` (default, ~300 ms) replays Google's own ``r06xKb`` batchexecute RPC on the Google Careers portal — clean JSON, 20 roles per page, scope = Google's internal openings. ``serp`` uses the public Jobs search vertical (``udm=8``, SERP-embedded, 3rd-party aggregator) and costs more latency because Google gates it behind JS.

get.description:
Search Google Shopping for products.

Requires browser rendering due to dynamic JS-loaded content.

get.summary: Resolve merchant URL for a Google Shopping product
get.description:
Resolve the real merchant URL for a product from Google Shopping.

Google has removed direct merchant links from organic Shopping HTML,
so the public /shopping/search response can only return the product
title + price + source. This endpoint takes a product title (and
optionally the source merchant) and uses Google's "I'm Feeling Lucky"
redirect (`btnI=1`) to materialize the top merchant URL, mirroring
the per-product enrichment pattern exposes via
`immersive product link`.

When `source` is passed, the query is scoped with `site:{domain}`
so the resolved URL lands on the exact merchant whose price was
shown in the card (rather than a generic top organic match).

Typical usage:
    1. Call GET /shopping/search?q=laptop
    2. Pick a product from the results
    3. Call the product's click_link field to get the merchant URL

Credits: 1 per call (one secondary scrape via curl_cffi).

parameters.name: title

schema.description: Exact product title from a search result
schema.title: Title

parameters.description: Exact product title from a search result

parameters.name: source

schema.description: Merchant source name from the shopping card (e.g. 'Walmart', 'Best Buy'). Forces the resolved URL to that merchant via site: operator.
schema.title: Source

parameters.description: Merchant source name from the shopping card (e.g. 'Walmart', 'Best Buy'). Forces the resolved URL to that merchant via site: operator.

get.description: Get detailed product information with seller offers.
Search Google Patents using the XHR API.
Get detailed patent information using the XHR endpoint (returns HTML).
Search Google Scholar for scholarly articles using direct HTTP.

Search Google Scholar for author profiles by name.

Google auth-gates the direct ``view_op=search_authors`` endpoint for
unauthenticated clients, so this handler falls back to a regular
``/scholar?q=<name>`` search and harvests unique
``/citations?user=<id>`` links from each result's author byline
(``gs_a`` div). The response schema is unchanged — each
``ScholarProfile`` carries ``author_id``, ``name``, and ``link``.
Affiliation, email domain, cited-by count, interests, and thumbnail
are intentionally left ``None`` because a search page doesn't carry
that metadata; callers who want them should pipe each ``author_id``
through ``/v1/google/scholar/author`` for the full profile.

``after_author`` / ``before_author`` pagination tokens are accepted
for API compatibility but ignored, since the fallback uses the main
Scholar search (which paginates via ``page=N``) rather than the
gated author-search view.

parameters.name: after_author

schema.description: Pagination token for the next page
schema.title: After Author

parameters.description: Pagination token for the next page

parameters.name: before_author

schema.description: Pagination token for the previous page
schema.title: Before Author

parameters.description: Pagination token for the previous page

get.description: Get detailed Google Scholar author profile including articles, stats, and co-authors.

get.summary: Get citation chart data for a Scholar author
get.description: Return the citations-per-year chart for a Google Scholar author.

get.summary: Get citation formats for a Scholar paper
get.description:
Return MLA, APA, Chicago, Harvard, and Vancouver citation formats.

**Currently returns HTTP 501.** Google's cite-dialog endpoint
(``/scholar?q=info:<cluster>:scholar.google.com/&output=cite``)
returns ``HTTP 403 Forbidden`` for every unauthenticated client as of
late 2026 — the dialog is only served to browsers that have an
active Scholar JS session established via a prior `/scholar` page
load. Working around that requires a stateful browser session which
is not cost-effective for a single citation lookup.

**Recommended workaround:** call ``/v1/google/scholar/search`` for
the paper and build a citation string client-side from the fields
it already returns: ``title``, ``authors`` (list), ``publication_info``
(which contains journal + year), and ``link``. That's enough to
assemble MLA / APA / Chicago / Harvard / Vancouver formats without
making a second HTTP hop.

Tracked in scrape-badger/scrapebadger#135 follow-up work; this
endpoint will be re-enabled when we add a browser-session-backed
fetch path.

parameters.name: q

schema.description: Cluster ID from a search result (e.g. '5Gohgn6QFikJ')
schema.title: Q

parameters.description: Cluster ID from a search result (e.g. '5Gohgn6QFikJ')

get.description: Get a stock or index quote from Google Finance.

Get AI-generated search results from Google AI Mode.

**Currently returns HTTP 501.** As of 2026 Google has repurposed
the historical ``udm=50`` parameter — it now opens the Visual Search
history prompt, not a dedicated AI Mode surface. There is no
standalone ``/search?udm=50`` URL that returns AI-only content
anymore; AI Overview blocks are rendered inside regular SERPs
instead, gated on query + user + geolocation signals.

**Recommended workaround:** call
``/v1/google/search?q=<query>&ai_overview=true``. The main SERP
endpoint already parses the ``ai_overview`` block when Google
includes one in the response, and the ``ai_overview=true`` flag
automatically chases the deferred ``page_token`` follow-up fetch
when Google serves the overview lazily. That's the exact data this
endpoint was originally meant to return.

This route remains registered so the product catalog, docs, and
SDKs don't break — it will be re-enabled if/when Google ships a
stable dedicated AI Mode surface again.
Search Google Lens by image URL for visually similar results.

parameters.name: url

schema.description: Public URL of the image to search visually
schema.title: Url

parameters.description: Public URL of the image to search visually

get.description:
Return Google Local Pack listings for a query.

Unlike ``/v1/google/maps/search`` (which targets the Maps Protobuf API and
is keyed by ``data_id``), this endpoint follows the Web SERP ``tbm=lcl``
mode — the same data that populates the "Places" block inside regular
search results. Use it when you care about ranking relative to a SERP
query (e.g. SEO/local-SEO research), not when you need full place
metadata.

Get deep product details from Google's immersive product page.

Two-step fetch: render the Shopping SERP for ``q`` to extract
session-bound tokens, then call ``/async/oapv`` with them. Returns
404 when the product isn't on the SERP — the caller's query has to
surface the tile because the tokens are session-scoped.

Response time: ~3 s warm (cached SERP), ~6 s cold (SERP + RPC).
Credits: 5 per call (matches the Shopping product-detail cost).

parameters.name: product_id

schema.description: Google Shopping product identifier (``gpcid``). Returned as ``product_id``/``gpcid`` on ``/shopping/search`` tiles.
schema.title: Product Id

parameters.description: Google Shopping product identifier (``gpcid``). Returned as ``product_id``/``gpcid`` on ``/shopping/search`` tiles.

parameters.name: q

schema.description: Original search query that surfaced the product. Required by Google's ``/async/oapv`` RPC — the page-level ``ei``/``xsrf`` and per-tile ``oapvfc`` tokens are extracted from the SERP Google renders for this exact query, so the product must be discoverable via this query.
schema.title: Q

parameters.description: Original search query that surfaced the product. Required by Google's ``/async/oapv`` RPC — the page-level ``ei``/``xsrf`` and per-tile ``oapvfc`` tokens are extracted from the SERP Google renders for this exact query, so the product must be discoverable via this query.

parameters.name: gl

schema.description: Country code (ISO 3166 alpha-2). Defaults to the country implied by ``domain`` (e.g. ``google.com.au`` → ``au``), falling back to ``us`` when domain doesn't carry a country hint.
schema.title: Gl

parameters.description: Country code (ISO 3166 alpha-2). Defaults to the country implied by ``domain`` (e.g. ``google.com.au`` → ``au``), falling back to ``us`` when domain doesn't carry a country hint.

parameters.name: domain

schema.description: Google domain ("google.com" / "google.co.uk" / …) — used to localise the SERP render that produces the session tokens.
schema.title: Domain

parameters.description: Google domain ("google.com" / "google.co.uk" / …) — used to localise the SERP render that produces the session tokens.

parameters.name: include_offers

schema.description: When true, return the full merchant-offer list with deep product URLs for every merchant Google ships an offer-block for. Offers come from the primary oapv response (top ~5 panel merchants: DICK'S, Kohl's, Zappos, etc.) plus a parallel paginated fan-out of ``/async/oapv`` with ``async_context=MORE_STORES`` at offsets sori=5..60 — which surfaces an additional ~50 sellers including GOAT, eBay sub-sellers, Macy's, StockX, Poshmark, Flight Club, etc. (+5 parallel RPCs, ~1 s wall-clock).
schema.title: Include Offers

parameters.description: When true, return the full merchant-offer list with deep product URLs for every merchant Google ships an offer-block for. Offers come from the primary oapv response (top ~5 panel merchants: DICK'S, Kohl's, Zappos, etc.) plus a parallel paginated fan-out of ``/async/oapv`` with ``async_context=MORE_STORES`` at offsets sori=5..60 — which surfaces an additional ~50 sellers including GOAT, eBay sub-sellers, Macy's, StockX, Poshmark, Flight Club, etc. (+5 parallel RPCs, ~1 s wall-clock).

parameters.name: include_variants

schema.description: When true, also fetch size/colour variants via ``/async/toy_v`` (+1 secondary RPC, ~1 s).
schema.title: Include Variants

parameters.description: When true, also fetch size/colour variants via ``/async/toy_v`` (+1 secondary RPC, ~1 s).

parameters.name: resolve_deep_urls

schema.description: When true (only meaningful with ``include_offers=true``), browser-render the Shopping SERP so additional merchant deep URLs surface from the rendered HTML. Best-effort: catalog-feed retailers (DSW / Famous Footwear / Shoe Carnival / Myer / rebel) get deep URLs; paid-Shopping-Ads merchants (Zappos, GOAT, Academy, etc.) still get their homepage because their click-through URLs are signed by Google's aclk redirect and can't be reproduced server-side. Adds ~5–8 s latency.
schema.title: Resolve Deep Urls

parameters.description: When true (only meaningful with ``include_offers=true``), browser-render the Shopping SERP so additional merchant deep URLs surface from the rendered HTML. Best-effort: catalog-feed retailers (DSW / Famous Footwear / Shoe Carnival / Myer / rebel) get deep URLs; paid-Shopping-Ads merchants (Zappos, GOAT, Academy, etc.) still get their homepage because their click-through URLs are signed by Google's aclk redirect and can't be reproduced server-side. Adds ~5–8 s latency.

get.description:
Return Google Shorts — short-form vertical videos surfaced in search.

Triggers Google's Shorts mode via `udm=39` and parses out the
`short_videos_results` carousel. Mostly returns YouTube Shorts but also
includes TikToks, Facebook Reels, and other short-form sources when
Google surfaces them.

Search Google Flights for available itineraries.

Returns `best_flights` (Google's top recommendations) and `other_flights`
(the rest of the result set), plus `price_insights` when Google shows
its typical-price range / high-low-typical indicator.

Parser is best-effort against the rendered DOM — Google's internal
batchexecute protobuf would give richer data (seat maps, booking tokens,
per-leg aircraft) but requires reverse-engineering that changes
frequently. The DOM route is more stable for daily-use scraping.

parameters.name: departure_id

schema.description: Departure airport IATA code (e.g. JFK) or location ID
schema.title: Departure Id

parameters.description: Departure airport IATA code (e.g. JFK) or location ID

parameters.name: arrival_id

schema.description: Arrival airport IATA code (e.g. LHR) or location ID
schema.title: Arrival Id

parameters.description: Arrival airport IATA code (e.g. LHR) or location ID

get.description: Health check endpoint.
API health check.

page_token.title: Page Token
page_token.description: Continuation token for the deferred AI Overview fetch. Non-null when Google embeds only a placeholder in the main SERP response.

deferred.title: Deferred
deferred.description: True when the AI Overview was present as a deferred block.

AiOverview.title: AiOverview
AiOverview.description:
Google AI Overview (AI-generated summary).

Google increasingly serves the AI Overview as a **deferred** block — the
initial SERP HTML contains only a placeholder plus a `page_token` that
the client has to POST back to retrieve the actual content. When that
happens, the SERP parser populates `page_token` and leaves
`text_blocks`/`references` empty. Callers of `/v1/google/search` can pass
`ai_overview=true` to have the scraper chase the token automatically and
merge the result back into this object.

AiOverviewListItem.title: AiOverviewListItem
AiOverviewListItem.description: A list item within an AI Overview text block.

AiOverviewReference.description: A source reference in AI Overview.

AiOverviewTextBlock.title: AiOverviewTextBlock
AiOverviewTextBlock.description: A typed content block within AI Overview.

AiReference.title: AiReference
AiReference.description: A reference cited in the AI response.

CitationByYear.title: CitationByYear
CitationByYear.description: One point in the citations-per-year chart.

FlightAirport.description: Airport metadata referenced in a search result.

carbon_emissions_diff_typical.description: Difference vs typical emissions for this route, in grams

FlightPriceInsights.title: FlightPriceInsights
FlightPriceInsights.description: Typical price range insight for the searched route.

HotelPrice.title: HotelPrice
HotelPrice.description: A hotel price from a specific source.

InlineSitelink.title: InlineSitelink
InlineSitelink.description: An inline sitelink (e.g. Reddit post metadata).

JobFilter.title: JobFilter
JobFilter.description: A filter option for job search.

via.title: Via
via.description: Source platform ("Talent.com", "Built In NYC", …) parsed from "via X".

apply_links.title: Apply Links
apply_links.description: Flat list of apply URLs for quick access.

MapsLocationDetails.title: MapsLocationDetails

image.title: Image
image.description: Full-size image URL (same CDN as thumbnail but larger)

image.title: Image
image.description: Full-size image URL (same CDN as thumbnail but larger)

MapsPlaceResult.description: A place from Maps search results.

has_updates.description: True when the SERP knowledge panel shows an 'Updates from' section

merchant_description.title: Merchant Description
merchant_description.description: The 'From the business' description text set by the business owner

NewsRelatedStory.title: NewsRelatedStory

ScholarAuthorArticle.title: ScholarAuthorArticle
ScholarAuthorArticle.description: One article row from the author profile's publication list.

ShoppingClickResponse.title: ShoppingClickResponse
ShoppingClickResponse.description:
Response for GET /api/v1/shopping/product/click.

Returns the direct merchant URL for a given product title by using
Google's "I'm Feeling Lucky" redirect. Mirrors 
immersive product link pattern — an on-demand per-product
enrichment that materializes the merchant link that Google strips from
organic Shopping HTML.

ShoppingProductResult.title: ShoppingProductResult
ShoppingProductResult.description: A single product from search results.

ShortVideoResult.title: ShortVideoResult
ShortVideoResult.description: A single short-form video result surfaced in Google Shorts.

Sitelink.title: Sitelink
Sitelink.description: A sitelink within an organic result.

SnippetLink.title: SnippetLink
SnippetLink.description: Inline link within an AI Overview text block.

TrendsAutocompleteItem.title: TrendsAutocompleteItem
TrendsAutocompleteItem.description:
A categorized topic suggestion returned by the Trends autocomplete API.

Distinct from Google Search autocomplete in that each entry is a
categorized entity with a Knowledge Graph `mid` identifier and a
semantic `type` ("Topic", "Sports", "Career", etc.) plus a direct
link into Google Trends.

TrendsSearchResponse.title: TrendsSearchResponse
TrendsSearchResponse.description:
Unified response for GET /api/v1/trends/search.

A single endpoint that dispatches on ``data_type``
(TIMESERIES, GEO_MAP, GEO_MAP_0, RELATED_TOPICS, RELATED_QUERIES).
Only the field set relevant to the requested ``data_type`` is
populated; others stay at their empty defaults.

TrendsTimelinePoint.title: TrendsTimelinePoint
TrendsTimelinePoint.description: A single point in interest over time.

TrendsTimelineValue.title: TrendsTimelineValue
TrendsTimelineValue.description: A single value in a trends timeline point.

TrendsTrendingArticle.title: TrendsTrendingArticle
TrendsTrendingArticle.description: An article associated with a trending search.
